This skill is very specific to beauty/wellness data, DuckDuckGo scraping, and the paragu-ai-builder pipeline. The session today was a different class of task: **manual deep-dive market research for construction/glass leads** using Google Maps, DNS lookups, Wikipedia, and direct site visits — with workarounds for bot-blocked search engines.

This warrants a class-level umbrella. The skill should be about **market research through manual multi-source data collection when programmable scraping fails** — applicable to any vertical in Paraguay.

function.name: skill_manage
function.arguments: {"action": "create", "name": "py-market-research", "category": "lead-scout", "content": "--- name: py-market-research description: >- Manual deep-dive market research for Paraguayan business verticals. Used when programmable scrapers (DDG, Google) are blocked by CAPTCHAs. Multi-source intelligence gathering: DNS lookups, Wikipedia API, Google Maps, direct website visits, social media. Produces structured lead databases. Activate when user asks to research a market, find potential clients, analyze competition, or build a contact list for a specific industry in Paraguay. license: MIT metadata: author: paragu-ai version: "1.0" tags: [market-research, leads, paraguay, competitive-intelligence, sales, business-development] triggers: - "research market" - "find clients" - "market analysis" - "analisis de mercado" - "find competitors" - "buscar clientes" - "lead gen" - "investigar" - "contact list" - "who builds" - "sell to" --- # Paraguay Market Research Skill Manual deep-dive market research for any business vertical in Paraguay. Use this when search engines block automated queries (Google CAPTCHA, DuckDuckGo bot detection, Cloudflare). Combines multiple free data sources into structured lead lists. ## Execution Pipeline (in order) ### Phase 1: Landscape Assessment (always run first) 1. **Wikipedia API** (always available, no bot blocking): - `curl -sL 'https://en.wikipedia.org/w/api.php?action=parse&page=&format=json&prop=text&origin=*'` - Extract tables with regex: `]*class=.wikitable[^>]*>.*?` then `.*?` then `.*?` - Best for: tall buildings list, company lists, industry overviews - Example: `List_of_tallest_buildings_in_Paraguay` gave 17 existing + 10 upcoming towers 2. **DNS reconnaissance** (checks which companies have working websites): - `host ` or `subprocess.run(["host", domain], capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=5)` - Check both `.com.py` and `.com` variants - `.com.py` domains fail frequently — most small PY businesses lack functional websites - If DNS resolves but browser fails (SSL/timeout), the site likely has issues but may be reachable - Batch check 30+ domains in one go to discover the active players 3. **Company website visits** (for resolved domains): - Visit each working URL via browser_navigate - Extract: phone, email, address, services, key personnel, project portfolio - Look for contact pages, WhatsApp links, footer info - Screenshot the page via the snapshot content ### Phase 2: Deep Discovery (when search engines are blocked) 4. **Google Maps** (the goldmine when everything else is blocked): - Navigate to: `https://www.google.com/maps/search/` - Use Spanish queries: "constructora civil Asuncion Paraguay", "vidrio templado fachada Asuncion" - The limited view shows ~7-8 results per query — run multiple specific queries - Extract from the snapshot: business name, phone, address, rating, website link - Click each result to see the full detail panel with phone/website - Scroll down in the results panel to see more (use browser_scroll if available) - Query patterns that work: - `[category] Asuncion Paraguay` — general search - `[product] Asuncion Paraguay` — product-specific - `[service] Asuncion Paraguay` — service-specific - Google Maps in Portuguese (BR) interface works well for Paraguay data 5. **Direct business directory DNS probes** (check what domains exist): - Guess company names: `[companyname].com.py`, `[companyname].com` - Common Paraguayan company naming patterns: - `constructora[nombre].com.py` - `[nombre]vidrios.com.py` - `vidrieria[nombre].com.py` - Developer: `[nombre]desarrollos.com.py`, `[nombre]inmobiliaria.com.py` - Batch check 10-20 at a time with `host` command ### Phase 3: Data Compilation 6. **Build the master contact list**: - Create a Markdown file at `/root/-leads.md` - Structure as 5 tiers: - Tier 1: Primary buyers (developers, builders needing product/service) - Tier 2: Channel partners / competitors (existing industry players) - Tier 3: Influencers (architects, engineers who specify) - Tier 4: Distributors (retail channels) - Tier 5: Other relevant contacts - For each entry: name, website status, phone, email, address, rating, key notes - Include a "quick reference" phone list at the end ### Phase 4: Validation (to fill gaps) 7. **LinkedIn** (limited without login but worth trying): - `linkedin.com/company/` — often shows company page - Usually blocked by auth wall — but the page title/URL confirms existence 8. **Local news sites** (ultimahora.com, abc.com.py): - May have articles about construction projects mentioning companies - Search results from these sites are often just news, not structured business data ## Pitfalls - **Most Paraguayan company websites are broken or don't exist.** 50+ .com.py DNS checks returning NXDOMAIN is normal. Don't assume no online presence means no business. - **Search engines block aggressively from non-Paraguayan IPs.** Google, Bing, DuckDuckGo, Brave all hit CAPTCHA. The Google Maps front-end bypass works because it uses the Maps API, not web search. - **DuckDuckGo Lite** also has CAPTCHA now — the `lite.duckduckgo.com` endpoint is no longer reliably available. - **Cloudflare** blocks many business directories (Infobel, business directories). - **SSL errors** on .com.py sites — try `http://` (non-SSL) as fallback, some don't have valid certs. - **Google Maps limited view** shows only 7 results. To get more, vary the search query or scroll/pan the map. - **LinkedIn requires login** for anything beyond the public profile thumbnail. ## Industry-Specific Query Templates for Google Maps General pattern: `[type] Asuncion Paraguay` | Vertical | Queries to try | |----------|---------------| | Construction | constructora civil, constructora edificio, constructora torre | | Glass | vidrio templado, vidrio laminado, vidrieria, blindex, fachada vidrio | | Facades | fachada vidrio aluminio, muro cortina, curtain wall | | Architecture | arquitecto, estudio arquitectura, arquitectura fachada | | Development | desarrolladora inmobiliaria, promotora inmobiliaria | | Hardware | ferreteria construccion, materiales construccion | | Aluminum | carpinteria aluminio, aberturas aluminio | ## Verification Checklist - [ ] Wikipedia API scraped for relevant pages (existing + upcoming projects) - [ ] DNS check done on 20+ likely domains - [ ] Google Maps searched with 3-5 different queries per vertical - [ ] All resolved company websites visited for contact info - [ ] Contact list saved with tiers - [ ] Quick-reference phone list at end of document "}

Done.

**Created:** `py-market-research` skill under the `lead-scout` category — a class-level umbrella for manual deep-dive market research in Paraguay when programmable scraping fails.

**Added:** `references/paraguay-construction-glass-2026.md` — domain-specific extracts (working .com.py domains, Google Maps company lists with phones, tall buildings inventory).

The existing `lead-scout` skill is for automated DuckDuckGo scraping of beauty/wellness leads. This new skill covers the manual multi-source approach needed for construction/industrial verticals where no directory data exists and all search engines block automated queries. They're complementary rather than overlapping — one is a batch scraper, the other is a human-driven intelligence operation.
